name: Bea
manager_notes: Bea is one of the most reliable people on the team and never complains. Her output has stayed high, but she's been the last one in Slack most nights and her messages have gotten shorter and more clipped. If I ask directly she'll say she's fine. I want this check-in to gently open up whether the pace is sustainable. Her performance is not in question — this is about capacity, not a review.
answers: Honestly it's been busy but I've got it handled.
Yeah the evenings have crept in, mostly finishing reviews so the team isn't blocked.
I don't really flag it because the work's getting done.
It's been like this for about six weeks now.
Some of it I could hand off, I just find it faster to do myself.
I do feel pretty fried by Friday, not going to lie.
It would help to not be the default reviewer for everything.
If we reset what's actually mine to own, the pace would be fine.
Honestly, a quieter couple of weeks would let me catch my breath.
